channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's veteran foreign minister has issued a veiled warning to America's new secretary of state: Behave yourself.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer And Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump visit is 'morale boost': CA fire official
item.description: "I don't know what Trump can give us, but it's a morale boost," said Cal Fire battalion chief Brent Pascua.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: Weeks after the worst of the deadly fires tore through Los Angeles suburbs, families are grappling with the reality that toxic ash from burned cars and homes could be a health issue for awhile, especially for children.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: A man in the central Oregon city of Bend says he was responsible for some of the googly eyes placed on sculptures there.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Target latest company to roll back DEI programs
item.description: Target has announced it is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, becoming the latest corporation to do so following President Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KTLA 5

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Defense Department orders 'pause' of social media accounts
item.description: The Defense Department said it needs time to review its social media programming to match Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Vance tells March for Life it's a 'blessing to fight for the unborn' during first week in office
item.description: JD Vance told the March for Life crowd Friday he wants to bring back a focus on families in the United States with President's Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man pleads guilty to assaulting American Airlines gate agent
item.description: A Florida man pleaded guilty in federal court after he assaulted an American Airlines gate agent at Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport, according to online court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: The man pushed onto New York's subway tracks says he will ride again
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man who survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an oncoming train said that in spite of the physical and psychological trauma, he eventually plans to make his way back to the train.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: 80 Guatemalan migrants deported from Biggs Army Airfield
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — The U.S. Department of Defense, along with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), deported 80 Guatemalan migrants on Friday morning, Jan. 24, from Biggs Army Airfield at Fort Bliss, U.S. Rep. Tony Gonzales, R-Texas said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Attorney Ben Crump files lawsuits against California power company
item.description: Lawsuits allege Southern California Edison failed to turn off utilities despite dangerous conditions.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: March for Life rally unfolding in Washington, with Vance to make first public appearance since inauguration
item.description: The annual March for Life rally is unfolding in Washington, D.C. on Friday with Vice President JD Vance set to make his first public appearance since being sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ford recalls over 270,000 Broncos and Mavericks: Here's why
item.description: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 272,827 Broncos and Mavericks due to a power problem that can cause vehicles to stop unexpectedly or fail to start.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tenn. Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve 3rd term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to tour wildfire damage in California, he's zeroing in on one of his frequent targets for criticism: State water policy. Since the fires broke out Jan.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James picked for 21st straight All-Star Game, extending NBA record
item.description: LeBron James is extending his All-Star records. And Giannis Antetokounmpo is the people’s choice, again. The NBA revealed the starters — some of them, anyway — for the revamped All-Star Game on Thursday night, and there wasn't much in the way of surprises.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Activists to 'resist' Trump through know-your-rights campaign
item.description: The pulse of El Paso’s immigrant community can be felt through the calls streaming into a small white “BNHR” building on Piedras Street over the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4
